one_time_description: English and Wabigoon Rivers Remediation Trust funding received ($4,042,889 in 2022 vs $1,999,262 in 2021) - remediation trust distributions
economic_notes: First Nation is economically dependent on federal transfers: 50% from ISC and 34% from FNIHB. Advances outstanding to Keewache-Ete-Min (Grassy Narrows Development Corporation) and 1737743 Ontario Inc. totaling $345,760. Subsequent event: US/Canada tariff uncertainty noted post year-end.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024 as template default). Two consolidated entities: Grassy Narrows First Nation and Grassy Narrows Education Authority. Government business entities excluded from consolidation. Large remediation trust revenues from English and Wabigoon Rivers Remediation Trust ($4.04M) related to mercury contamination remediation - treated as one-time/non-recurring. First Nation non-compliant with First Nations Financial Transparency Act posting deadline. Audit report dated June 24, 2025 - unusually late audit completion. Net book value of TCA derived from Schedule 1 total: $27,944,519 (Schedule 1 shows $28,101,602 for 2022 net book value). Tangible capital assets include $8,995,707 in construction-in-progress not yet amortized. No schedule of remuneration included in the provided financial statements. OSR calculated as total revenue $30,085,435 minus total government transfers $28,664,522 = $1,420,913.
